items.title: Virtual dispatch and abstract interfaces: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? Any same-named derived function is dynamically selected even when the base function is not virtual or the object was copied by value.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Runtime dispatch requires a virtual base function and access through a base reference or pointer to an unsliced object.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Runtime dispatch requires a virtual base function and access through a base reference or pointer to an unsliced object.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Runtime dispatch requires a virtual base function and access through a base reference or pointer to an unsliced object.
Any same-named derived function is dynamically selected even when the base function is not virtual or the object was copied by value.

answer.explanation: Runtime dispatch requires a virtual base function and access through a base reference or pointer to an unsliced object.

items.title: Virtual dispatch and abstract interfaces: executable application
items.prompt: Define Shape with a virtual area query and virtual destructor, implement Square and Rectangle, select one from input through unique_ptr<Shape>, and print its area.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
